name: Kung Pao Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 1 Whole chicken breast, skinned and boned
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into thin strips.
2. In a small bowl, combine 1 tablespoon cornstarch and 1 tablespoon teriyaki sauce with ground red pepper. Stir in the chicken. Let stand for 15 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, in another small bowl, mix water, remaining 1 tablespoon cornstarch, and 1 tablespoon teriyaki sauce with vinegar.
4. Separate and rinse the lettuce; pat dry. Cut the leaves crosswise into 2-inch strips.
5. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a hot wok or large skillet over high heat. Add the chicken and stir-fry for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
6. Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon oil in the same pan. Add the lettuce and stir-fry for 1 minute.
7. Stir in the chicken and teriyaki sauce mixture. Cook until the mixture boils and thickens.
8. Remove from heat and stir in the roasted peanuts. Serve immediately.
Note: Consider substituting onion pieces for the lettuce, perhaps 1 large onion cut into 1/4-inch pieces.
Serves: 4
